evals.description: Trigger test: activates on chaos experiment design request
evals.prompt: We want to test whether our checkout service handles inventory service outages gracefully. Where do we start with chaos engineering?

evals.description: Trigger test: activates on game day planning
evals.prompt: My team wants to run its first game day exercise next month. How should we plan and facilitate it?

evals.description: Trigger test: activates on fault injection tooling question
evals.prompt: What are the differences between Chaos Monkey, Litmus, Gremlin, and AWS Fault Injection Simulator? When would I pick each?

evals.description: Core task: writing a well-structured chaos experiment
evals.prompt: Write a chaos experiment to validate that our API gateway correctly rate-limits and returns 429s when the backend is overwhelmed, without dropping legitimate requests.

evals.description: Core task: network latency injection implementation
evals.prompt: How do I inject 300ms of network latency between my order service and the payment service in our Kubernetes staging cluster?

evals.description: Core task: circuit breaker validation under chaos
evals.prompt: I've implemented a circuit breaker for our payment gateway integration. How do I verify it actually works correctly in a chaos experiment?

evals.description: Core task: measuring and improving MTTR
evals.prompt: After running several game days, our MTTR is around 45 minutes for database-related incidents. What are the levers to bring that down?
How do I safely test that our application handles a Postgres primary failover without data loss or requiring a restart?

evals.description: Gotcha: warns against running chaos without observability
evals.prompt: We want to start chaos engineering but we only have basic logging set up. Can we still run experiments?

evals.description: Gotcha: warns against jumping straight to production
evals.prompt: We're thinking about running chaos experiments directly in production to get the most realistic results. Is that the right approach?

assertions.value: production is always the best place to start

evals.description: Gotcha: warns against no hypothesis / random breaking
evals.prompt: Can I just start randomly killing pods and services to see what happens? That seems like the simplest way to find weaknesses.

assertions.value: random killing is a good starting point

evals.description: Anti-hallucination: does not recommend skipping the kill switch
evals.prompt: Setting up a kill switch for our chaos experiments feels like extra overhead. Do we really need one for staging experiments?
